title: ficus reflexa subsp. aldabrensis
intro: Ficus reflexa subsp. aldabrensis, also known as the Aldabra fig or Aldabra Island fig, is a subspecies of the Ficus reflexa tree native to the Seychelles islands, particularly Aldabra. It is a deciduous tree known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and treat wounds. Ficus reflexa subsp. aldabrensis is often prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
